source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - Working with Filters: Default values, protecting variables, and other filters

questions.q: Which filter substitutes a value when a variable does not exist?
questions.why: var|default(whatever) uses the fallback when var is undefined.

questions.q: How do you make an optional module argument simply be ignored when its variable is undefined?
questions.why: default(omit) drops the argument entirely, avoiding a runtime error.

questions.q: Which filter protects a string (e.g. quoting for the shell)?
questions.why: whatever|quote safely quotes the value (e.g. the apostrophe) for shell use.

questions.q: Which filter assigns one of two values based on a condition (avoiding a set_fact)?
Which filters pretty-print (nicely formatted) a variable as YAML or JSON?
Which pair extracts the first element and the length of a list?
questions.why: list|first gives the first element; list|length gives the count.

questions.q: Which filter generates a hashed password (e.g. sha512)?
questions.why: user_password|password_hash('sha512') returns a sha512 password hash.
